meta.title: React Props – Complete Guide to Passing Data (2026)
meta.description: Master React props for component communication. Learn prop types, default props, children prop, prop drilling, and best practices.

content.value: Props (short for properties) are read-only data passed from parent to child components. They allow components to be dynamic and reusable. Props are immutable – components cannot modify their own props.

value: **Keep props few** – Aim for 3-5 props per component
**Avoid spreading unknown props** – Can cause unexpected behavior
**Use meaningful names** – Props should be self-documenting
**Use children for composition** – More flexible than multiple props

content.value: Props are essential for React component communication. They make components reusable and predictable. Use TypeScript or PropTypes for type safety, children for composition, and lift state up when needed. Avoid prop drilling by using Context or component composition.

faq.question: Can props be changed?
faq.answer: No, props are read-only. Components cannot modify their own props.

faq.question: Props vs state?
faq.answer: Props are passed from parent and immutable; state is internal and mutable.

faq.question: What is prop drilling?
faq.answer: Passing props through multiple intermediate components that don't need them.

faq.question: How to set default props?
faq.answer: Use destructuring default values: function Button({ size = 'medium' })
